text: Data in transit is encrypted using TLS 1.3 with certificate validation. At rest, we use AES-256-GCM. Customers can use customer-managed keys (CMK) via AWS KMS/Azure Key Vault/HashiCorp Vault, with full control over key rotation and revocation. We cannot decrypt data without customer authorization.

We support end-to-end encryption where data is encrypted client-side before transmission and only decrypted on authorized endpoints. Our architecture is zero-knowledge, meaning we cannot access plaintext data even for support operations.

Key management includes strict lifecycle policies: generation, rotation, revocation, and destruction. Access to keys is tightly controlled via IAM policies, with audit logging for all key usage. Customers can revoke keys at any time, rendering stored data inaccessible.

We offer both provider-managed and customer-managed key options. Responsibilities are clearly defined: provider-managed keys simplify operations, while CMKs give customers full control. All models include strong encryption and auditable access controls.
We encrypt data in transit and at rest, but do not specify exact protocols, algorithms, or key management details.
We manage encryption keys on behalf of customers, but details about access, rotation, and control boundaries are not clearly defined.
We rotate encryption keys periodically, though policies and processes are not fully documented or exposed to customers.
Encryption is implemented, but it is not स्पष्ट whether internal teams can access or decrypt customer data.
We use military-grade encryption to protect all customer data.
We use 256-bit encryption without specifying algorithms, modes, or key management.
We handle all encryption and key management so customers do not need to worry about it.
We are unable to explain how encryption keys are generated, stored, or controlled.
